title: Research
text: Our research is organized around the complete optical measurement chain. The descriptions below are intentionally broad pending institutional review; they do not claim clinical validation, funding or specific performance outcomes.

title: Research
text:
Ophthalmic instrumentation
We explore optical and optoelectronic concepts for research instruments that interrogate or image the eye. Relevant work may combine illumination design, sensing geometry, electronic acquisition and computational analysis.

title: Research
text:
Biomedical optical imaging
We study imaging systems in which optical architecture, acquisition strategy and computation are designed together around a biomedical measurement question.

title: Research
text:
Wearable optical sensing
This area considers compact optical interfaces, motion-aware acquisition, embedded electronics and signal processing for wearable research platforms.

title: Research
text:
Optoelectronic instrumentation
We develop experimental measurement chains that connect light sources, detectors, analog and digital electronics, control software and data interpretation.

title: Research
text:
Embedded acquisition systems
Embedded platforms support synchronized acquisition, experimental control, communication and rapid prototyping. Readable protocols and inspectable software are part of the research workflow.

title: Research
text:
Colorimetry and spectral sensing
We investigate colorimetric, photometric, radiometric and multispectral measurement approaches, with attention to sensor response and calibration.

title: Research
text:
Computational imaging and artificial intelligence
We explore computational approaches for reconstruction, feature extraction and interpretation of optical data. Any future claims about datasets, models or performance will be added only after verification.






Note



The official laboratory name, extended title, institutional description and detailed research portfolio remain to be confirmed. See CONTENT-TODO.md.

title: Wearable Optical Sensing Platform
text: Demonstrative content — replace before institutional release

title: Wearable Optical Sensing Platform
text:
Research context
Wearable optical sensing brings together tissue–light interaction, optomechanical interfaces, low-noise acquisition, embedded processing and robust signal interpretation.

title: Wearable Optical Sensing Platform
text:
Demonstrator scope
This page reserves a structure for a future, publishable OptoLAB project. No device specification, study, funding source, clinical claim or quantitative result is implied.

title: Wearable Optical Sensing Platform
text:
Before publication
Add only reviewed information: the research question, responsible team, institutional partners, approved images, project status and links to public outputs.

title: Embedded Camera Acquisition
text: Demonstrative content — replace before institutional release

title: Embedded Camera Acquisition
text:
Research context
Embedded cameras provide a compact platform for exploring how sensor configuration, memory, image compression and transfer protocols affect an acquisition chain.

title: Embedded Camera Acquisition
text:
Demonstrator scope
This demonstrator links ESP32-S3 firmware with a PySide6 desktop interface over a readable TCP protocol. The public educational resource supports live JPEG streaming, JSON control commands and still-image capture.

title: Embedded Camera Acquisition
text:
Related public resource
See esp32-s3-camera-gui for the verified public description, source code and teaching notes.

title: OptoLAB
text:
University of Modena and Reggio Emilia




Optical technologies for biomedical imaging, sensing and ophthalmic instrumentation.


OptoLAB brings together optics, electronics, embedded systems and computation to explore new research instruments—from the first photon at the sensor to interpretable data.


Explore our research Contact the lab







Abstract illustration of spectral light beams crossing optical elements and converging on a sensor

title: OptoLAB
text:
Across optics, instruments and computation
We investigate measurement chains as connected systems: optical interaction, sensor response, electronic acquisition and computational interpretation.

title: OptoLAB
section: From measurement idea to research demonstrator
text:
From measurement idea to research demonstrator
These provisional examples describe technology directions, not funded projects or validated outcomes. They will be replaced as institutional content is reviewed.

title: OptoLAB
text:
Building the complete measurement chain
Our work spans the interfaces between light, hardware and information. The current capability map is intentionally qualitative while facilities and equipment records are being verified.

title: OptoLAB
section: Inspectable tools for learning and research
text:
Inspectable tools for learning and research
Public repositories make methods easier to examine, teach and extend. Explore verified OptoLAB resources for sensor calibration, embedded camera acquisition and serial instrumentation.
Browse software & resources Visit GitHub

title: Facilities & Capabilities
text: OptoLAB works across optical, electronic and computational prototyping. A verified equipment inventory is being prepared; no specific instrument, performance range or access condition is claimed on this page.

title: Facilities & Capabilities
text:
Equipment information to verify
Before this page becomes an equipment catalogue, each entry must include an approved name, manufacturer and model where appropriate, measurement purpose, key verified specifications, access conditions, responsible contact and an authorized image.

title: Multichannel Sensor Calibration
text: Demonstrative content — replace before institutional release

title: Multichannel Sensor Calibration
text:
Research context
Compact multi-channel sensors can support photometric and radiometric measurements when their spectral response and calibration chain are treated explicitly.

title: Multichannel Sensor Calibration
text:
Demonstrator scope
This placeholder describes a possible workflow spanning reference measurements, calibration model development and verification on independent observations. It does not state performance figures or completed validation.

title: Multichannel Sensor Calibration
text:
Related public resource
The public repository Calibration-Method-for-Multi-Channel-Sensor is intended to host code related to a paper on flexible calibration methods for low-cost photometric and radiometric measurements.

title: Publications
text: This page is ready for a verified BibTeX bibliography. No publications are listed yet because authorship, affiliations and bibliographic records must be reviewed before release.

title: Publications
text:
Add a publication
Add a valid record to publications.bib, then cite it from this page with its BibTeX key. The repository README documents the workflow.






Important



Do not publish placeholder citations or infer laboratory authorship from repository metadata.

title: Software & Resources
text: The following cards are based only on public repository descriptions and README files retrieved from the OPTOLAB-UNIMORE GitHub organization.

title: Software & Resources
text:
Calibration Method for Multi-Channel Sensor
Repository intended to contain code related to work on flexible calibration methods for low-cost photometric and radiometric measurements.
Open repository →

title: Software & Resources
text:
ESP32-S3 Camera GUI
Educational PySide6 GUI and ESP32-S3 firmware for Wi-Fi camera streaming, TCP control and high-resolution snapshot capture.
Open repository →

title: Software & Resources
text:
Educational Serial Monitor GUI
Educational PySide6 desktop GUI for serial device control, live analog signal plotting, peak detection, data inspection and Arduino/ESP32 firmware examples.
Open repository →

title: Software & Resources
text:
Responsible reuse
Each repository is the authoritative source for its requirements, setup instructions and current status. Review its README and license information before reuse.
